message:
fix(runtime): Comprehensive type comparison fixes for float test

This commit extends pyEqual and related functions to handle all
Python comparison edge cases, enabling the float test to pass.

## Key Fixes

### 1. Builtin Subclass Handling (pyEqual)
Classes inheriting from float/int/str have __base_value__ field.
pyEqual now extracts this value for comparison:
- FloatSubclass(42.0) == 42.0 now works correctly

### 2. BigInt vs i64 Comparison (PyValue.eql)
Added special case for BigInt/int comparison in PyValue.eql():
- Uses BigInt.eqlInt() for precision-preserving comparison
- Added BigInt to numeric coercion (converts via toFloat())

### 3. BigInt.eqlInt Method (bigint.zig)
New method to compare BigInt with i64 directly:
- Converts BigInt to i64 if possible, compares
- Returns false if BigInt is too large for i64

### 4. NaN Identity Semantics (pyAnyEql, pyEqual)
Python containers use identity check for element comparison.
[nan] == [nan] returns True when nan is same object.
- Float comparison now uses bit-level equality
- Struct comparison uses recursive pyAnyEql for NaN handling

### 5. String Literal Handling (toPyValue)
Added handling for *const [N:0]u8 (string literals):
- Converts to []const u8 slice for PyValue.string

### 6. toPyValue BigInt Support
Added BigInt handling at start of toPyValue function.

## Files Modified
- packages/runtime/src/runtime/builtins.zig - pyEqual improvements
- packages/runtime/src/runtime.zig - pyAnyEql NaN handling
- packages/runtime/src/Objects/object.zig - toPyValue, PyValue.eql
- packages/bigint/src/bigint.zig - eqlInt method

Tests passing: int_literal, unary, longexp, binop, bool, float (6 tests)
